text.title: gitlab-boards Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gitlab-boards as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Putting quick-action strings like "/label ~workflow::review" in a PUT request body to update issue description.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Quick actions only parse on initial POST creation or comment creation. PUTs to description do not re-trigger quick-action parsing, so the label is silently ignored.

failure-mode.description: Using Personal Access Tokens with wide scopes for bot operations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prefer Group or Project Access Tokens scoped to "api" + "write_repository" only. Wide-scope PATs create blast radius on compromise.

failure-mode.description: Bot-created issues notifying all project subscribers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bot users create issues as bot-owned; downstream notifications fan out to thousands. Use a dedicated bot user with notifications muted.

failure-mode.description: Assuming free-tier feature parity for iteration cadences.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Iteration cadence creation requires Premium. Agents written against Premium features break on free-tier customer projects.
